text: IDF struck Tofiq Daru pharmaceutical factory in Tehran, claiming it supplied fentanyl to Iran's SPND for chemical weapons R&D. Major new claim — essentially accusing Iran of violating the Chemical Weapons Convention. Not independently verified. Iran has historically denied CW programs.
verificationNote: Israel National News and Times of Israel confirmed IDF statement. CW claim unverified.

text: White House confirms Trump to address the nation Wednesday 9 PM ET on Iran. Follows his statement that US forces could leave in '2-3 weeks.' Combined with Pezeshkian's 'necessary will' to end war and Pakistan hosting direct talks, signals from both sides suggest potential off-ramp emerging.
verificationNote: CNBC, Axios, Bloomberg confirmed address. NPR confirmed '2-3 weeks' quote.

text: IDF dual airstrike in Beirut — Khalde highway + Jnah area. 7 killed, 24 wounded per Lebanese Health Ministry. IDF says targeted senior Hezbollah commander + another operative. Hezbollah confirmed one commander was killed. Ongoing Israeli ground ops south of Litani deepening.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, PBS, Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed.

text: Araghchi on Al Jazeera: 'Trust level is at zero.' Confirms receiving Witkoff messages but denies sending response to US 15-point plan. Wants 'complete end to war' not ceasefire. Contradicts Hegseth/Rubio claims that negotiations are 'very real' and 'gaining strength.'
verificationNote: Al Jazeera interview confirmed. Anadolu Agency corroborated.

text: Allied tensions escalating: France refused Israeli resupply overflight, Italy denied Sigonella access, Spain closed airspace to US planes. Rubio warned of NATO reassessment post-war. Fox News internal divisions — Laura Ingraham reportedly breaking ranks on war support.
verificationNote: CBS News, Stars and Stripes, Media Matters confirmed.

text: Iran threatens 17 US tech companies (Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, IBM, Intel, Tesla + 10 more) if more Iranian leaders killed. Retaliation threat issued via Fars news agency. Represents escalation from military to tech/economic targeting.
verificationNote: Times of Israel reported. Fars News Agency statement referenced.

text: Day 32 PM evening summary (7:25 PM PDT): (1) IDF strikes Tofiq Daru chemical factory; (2) Trump to address nation Wed 9PM; (3) IDF dual Beirut strike kills Hezb commander; (4) Araghchi: 'zero trust'; (5) Pope: 'atrocious' war; (6) Allied overflight tensions; (7) Pakistan hosting talks; (8) Iran threatens 17 tech companies; (9) Oil Brent $108/WTI $101.38; (10) Gas $4.018.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, PBS, Axios, CBS News, NPR.
